title: Frontend Descriptor for Playwright Validator
description: Declarative descriptor for a frontend surface to be validated by the Playwright behavioral validator (skills/playwright-validator/). Consumed by the Playwright runner to discover the surface, drive auth flows, resolve selector aliases, and select the browser matrix.

schema_version.description: Descriptor schema version. Reserved for future-proofing.

name.description: Human-readable name of the frontend surface.

base_url.description: Base URL where the frontend is reachable during validation. May be a localhost URL when validate-feature has deployed the service locally.

lifecycle.description: How to start and stop the frontend if not externally managed.

startup_command.description: Shell command to start the frontend (e.g., 'python -m http.server 8000').

startup_cwd.description: Working directory for startup_command, relative to repo root.

teardown_command.description: Optional cleanup command after validation completes.

auth_flow.description: Ordered list of Playwright actions to authenticate before scenarios run. Empty array means no auth needed.

selector.description: Selector alias from the selectors map, OR a literal Playwright selector.

value.description: For 'fill' actions: the value to type. May reference env vars as ${VAR_NAME}.

url.description: For 'goto' actions: the destination path or full URL.

selectors.description: Map of friendly selector aliases to literal Playwright selectors. Scenarios reference these aliases instead of brittle CSS paths so descriptor edits don't require rewriting scenarios.

browsers.description: Browser engines to execute scenarios against. Findings include a metadata.browser field identifying which engine produced them.

test_isolation.description: Whether each generated test gets a fresh browser context.

openspec_change_default.description: Default OpenSpec change-id to seed scenarios from. May be overridden at invocation time.
